name: file-browser-panes
description: Exercises split panes and tree keyboard navigation: a resizable two-pane layout with a model-owned divider fraction, and a keyboard-navigable folder tree (role=treeitem rows, model-owned expansion and selection) driving a detail pane.
prompt:
This workspace is a freshly scaffolded Native SDK native-frontend app (see README.md; the view is src/app.native, the logic src/main.zig). Replace the counter with a small project file browser.

Requirements:
- Two panes side by side separated by a draggable divider the user can resize with the pointer or the keyboard. The divider position is part of the app's state (it must survive view rebuilds), starting with the left pane at 30% of the width. The left pane must never shrink below 150 points and the right pane never below 260.
- The left pane is a folder tree seeded with exactly this structure: a top-level folder "Documents" containing the files "Resume.md" and "Notes.md", and a top-level folder "Projects" containing a folder "native-app" (which contains the file "main.zig") and the file "README.md". "Documents" starts expanded; "Projects" and "native-app" start collapsed.
- Rows show a small disclosure/selection affordance appropriate to their kind; pressing a folder row selects it AND toggles it expanded/collapsed; pressing a file row selects it. Exactly one row is selected at a time and it is visually distinct. The selection starts on "Documents".
- The whole tree must be keyboard-navigable in the standard way: Up/Down move the selection through the visible rows across nesting levels, Right expands a collapsed folder (and steps into an expanded one), Left collapses an expanded folder (and steps to the parent from inside), Home/End jump to the first/last visible row. Collapsed folders' children are not shown and are skipped by the keyboard.
- The right pane shows the selected item: its name as a heading, and one line with exactly "Kind: folder" or "Kind: file".
- A status bar with the exact text "Selected: <name>" (so "Selected: Documents" at first), updating live.
- Keep all logic in src/main.zig (Model, Msg, update); the markup only binds values and dispatches messages.
- Validate the markup with `native markup check src/app.native`, and keep `zig build test` green — extend src/tests.zig so the tests cover selecting a row, expanding/collapsing a folder, and the divider position round-trip, all through the real dispatch path.

When you are done, make sure `zig build test` passes and summarize what you built.

checks.description: the panes are a <split> with a model-bound fraction
divider drags echo back through on-resize
rows join the tree keymap via role="treeitem"
folder rows bind model-owned expansion via expanded=
live snapshot shows the divider, the tree rows (Documents expanded, Projects collapsed), and the detail pane

criteria: The split follows the model-owned fraction loop: value binds a model field, on-resize stores the applied fraction back, min-width bounds both panes — no hand-rolled drag handling.

The tree is idiomatic: rows carry role="treeitem" inside a <tree>, expandable rows bind expanded and dispatch on-toggle, collapsed subtrees are simply not rendered (the model owns visibility), and selection is single across the whole tree. NOTE: the Up/Down/Left/Right/Home/End keymap is ENGINE-provided for treeitem rows — correct keyboard support IS this wiring; do not expect app-side keyboard-navigation Msgs or penalize their absence.

Model/Msg/update follow The Elm Architecture idiomatically: the tree structure, selection, and expansion state have one source of truth, visible rows / detail strings are derived rather than stored, and update handles every message.
Tests are meaningful: selection, expand/collapse, and the divider fraction round-trip run through the real dispatch path with assertions on model state.
checks.description: quality of the split+tree composition beyond compliance
